title: Analytics - Device Added
scenario: Analytics event for successful device pairing does not display expected default elements (Device Name, Device PID, Device Serial) in Firebase dashboard
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Ensure no device is currently paired (if a device is paired, go to Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Forget Device).
3. Put the Poly Bluetooth audio device into pairing mode (press and hold the Power button or Bluetooth button until the LED flashes).
4. On the Poly Studio Mobile Home screen, tap "Pair New Device" or select the device from the available devices list.
5. Complete the pairing process. Wait for the confirmation that the device is successfully paired (e.g., "Device Connected" or similar status on the Home screen).
6. Navigate to the Firebase Analytics console and open the page for the pairing success event (e.g., "device_paired" or similar event name).
7. Verify that the following default elements are displayed: graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by Country/Gender, events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment.
8. Verify that the following custom parameters are also displayed: Device Name, Device PID, and Device Serial.
9. Wait a few hours (as per Firebase data processing latency).
10. Return to the Firebase Analytics console and check the event count for the pairing success event.
11. Verify that the event count for related elements (e.g., Device Name, Device PID, Device Serial) has increased.
expected: After pairing a new device, the Firebase Analytics page for the pairing success event displays default elements (graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by Country/Gender, events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment) plus custom parameters Device Name, Device PID, and Device Serial. After a few hours, the event count for these elements increases.
